ach: Faker is widely regarded as the greatest League of Legends player of all time, having won four World Championship titles (2013, 2015, 2016, 2023) with T1. He is the only player to have competed at Worlds for over a decade at an elite level, earning the title 'The Unkillable Demon King.'

Ninja became the most-followed Twitch streamer in history with over 18 million followers and was the face of Fortnite at its cultural peak in 2018. His stream with Drake set a concurrent viewer record for a non-tournament broadcast, and he moved to Mixer in a $30 million deal before returning to Twitch.

Fatal1ty was one of the first professional esports players to earn a six-figure income from gaming, winning 12 Major Championships across Quake III Arena and Painkiller. He pioneered the concept of professional FPS gaming before esports was an established industry and became the first gamer to have his own branded hardware line.

Daigo Umehara holds the Guinness World Record as the most successful Street Fighter player of all time. His 2004 EVO parry sequence against Justin Wong - dubbed 'EVO Moment 37' - is the most watched competitive gaming clip in history and is cited as the moment that legitimized fighting games as a spectator sport.

s1mple is considered the greatest Counter-Strike player of all time, having won HLTV's Player of the Year award four times (2018, 2021, 2022, 2023). His mechanical skill, particularly his AWP sniping, is considered technically unmatched in the history of the game.
wi: Received a VAC ban in 2014 at age 16 for using cheat software on a non-competitive CS:GO server. He served a 1-year competitive ban before building his legitimate career as the world's best player.

ach: ZywOo won HLTV's Player of the Year award in 2019 and 2020, becoming only the second player besides s1mple to win it, and is considered s1mple's only legitimate rival for the title of best CS player ever. His consistent performances for Vitality made him one of France's most celebrated sports figures.

NiKo is one of the most consistently high-performing riflers in CS:GO history, having spent years ranked in the HLTV top 5 with teams including FaZe Clan and G2 Esports. Despite his individual brilliance, his long pursuit of a Major Championship defined a narrative about peak individual skill without team success.

dev1ce is the most decorated Major Championship winner in CS:GO history, having won four Majors with Astralis. His methodical AWP play and team-first mentality made Astralis the most dominant team in CS:GO history during their 2018-2019 peak, holding the world number one ranking for over 300 consecutive days.

GeT_RiGhT was the definitive Counter-Strike player of the transitional era between 1.6 and CS:GO, winning the first Major Championship with NiP and reaching a legendary 87-0 map win streak at the start of CS:GO's competitive history. He is widely credited with establishing the philosophy of the support rifler in CS.

sp: Marvel vs. Capcom / Mortal Kombat / Street Fighter
ach: Justin Wong won eight EVO Championship titles across multiple fighting games and is known for his versatility across nearly every major fighting franchise. He was Daigo Umehara's opponent in EVO Moment 37 and has remained at the top of competitive fighting for over 20 years.

PewDiePie was the most subscribed YouTube channel in the world for multiple years between 2013 and 2019, pioneering the Let's Play commentary format that became YouTube's dominant gaming content style. At his peak he influenced game sales more than any gaming publication, and his playthroughs defined indie horror discovery.
wi: Documented controversies: paid users in 2017 to hold an antisemitic sign ('Death to all Jews'), losing his Disney partnership, and used a racial slur live on stream the same year.

ach: Shroud was a professional CS:GO player for Cloud9 before transitioning to full-time streaming, where his inhuman mechanical precision in games including PUBG, Valorant, and Apex Legends earned him a reputation as the most accurate aimer on Twitch. He signed a $10 million deal with Mixer before returning to Twitch.

xQc was a professional Overwatch League player for the Dallas Fuel before becoming Twitch's most-watched streamer by total hours viewed, surpassing all competitors including Ninja. His unfiltered commentary, frequent controversial statements, and marathon streaming sessions attract millions of daily viewers.
wi: Dropped from the Overwatch League Dallas Fuel in 2018 for using a homophobic slur on stream. Also penalized for stream sniping and has been criticized for promoting gambling content to a young audience.

ach: Pokimane became the most followed female streamer on Twitch, building a massive audience across Fortnite, League of Legends, and variety content. She is one of the co-founders of the content creation company Offline TV and has been among Forbes' Top Creators lists multiple times.

Dendi was the most celebrated Dota 2 player of the early competitive era, winning TI1 with Na'Vi and finishing second at TI2. He is best known for his flamboyant Pudge and Invoker plays that made him a fan favorite worldwide. His presence at events electrified audiences and gave Dota 2 its first true celebrity.

N0tail won The International back-to-back with OG in 2018 and 2019, making OG the only team to win The International twice. He was the highest cumulative prize money earner in esports history at multiple points, and his team's underdog 2019 TI run is considered one of esports' greatest stories.

KuroKy appeared at The International every year from 2011 through 2019 and won TI7 as captain of Team Liquid. He holds records for most TI appearances and most TI prize money earnings among support players, and his longevity at the top of Dota 2 for nearly a decade is unmatched.

Puppey is the only player to have attended every single edition of The International from TI1 through TI11, making him the most consistent Dota 2 competitive presence in history. He captained Na'Vi to a TI1 win and has led Team Secret to multiple Valve Major victories across a decade of competition.

Bugha won the Fortnite World Cup Solo event in 2019 at the age of 16, earning $3 million - the largest single-event prize ever awarded to an individual esports player at the time. His dominant performance across the multi-day event, going largely unmatched by professional players from across the globe, shocked the industry.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: MkLeo is widely considered the best Super Smash Bros. Ultimate player in the world, having won multiple major championship events and Smash World Tour championships. His dominance of Ultimate elevated Mexican players' global recognition in the fighting game community.

Armada was ranked the number one Smash Bros. Melee player in the world multiple times and is considered one of the greatest Melee players in the game's history. His patient, analytical playstyle with Peach and Young Link contrasted with the more aggressive American scene and made him a transcontinental figure in the community.

Mango won two EVO Championship titles for Melee (2014, 2019) and multiple Genesis championships, cementing his place as one of the most successful and beloved Smash players ever. His aggressive, style-first approach and charismatic personality made him the most popular personality in the Smash community for over 15 years.

Doublelift was the longest-tenured player in North American League of Legends history, competing professionally from 2011 through 2020, and won a record seven LCS titles with multiple teams. He is considered the greatest North American ADC in LoL history and was famous for his trash talk as much as his gameplay.

Bjergsen was the dominant mid laner in North American LoL for nearly a decade with TSM, winning six LCS titles and setting LCS records for kills and assist totals. He later moved into coaching and management, helping shape the next generation of North American LoL talent.

Caps won multiple LEC titles with G2 Esports and reached multiple World Championship finals, consistently ranked among Europe's best mid laners. His aggressive carry playstyle earned him the nickname 'Baby Faker' from Korean fans during his first Worlds appearance at just 17 years old.

Uzi reached the World Championship final twice with Royal Never Give Up and is universally considered the greatest ADC player in League of Legends history. His mechanical ceiling was described by analysts and professionals as exceeding that of any other player at the position, though injuries forced his retirement at just 22.

Serral became the first non-Korean player to win the GSL Super Tournament on Korean soil in 2018, an achievement many considered impossible given South Korean domination of StarCraft II. He followed this with a WCS Global Finals victory in 2018 and became the only Western player to reach world number one in SC2.

Flash is considered the greatest StarCraft: Brood War player of all time, nicknamed 'The God' for his perfect macro, unmatched multitasking, and mechanical skill. He dominated Korean competitive play from 2007 through the end of the Brood War progaming era, winning four OSL titles and three MSL titles.

Boxer was the first esports superstar, nicknamed 'The Terran Emperor' for his innovative Terran play that popularized early aggression with the Marine and Medic combo. He won the first two OSL championships and is credited with creating the Korean esports celebrity culture and team-based progaming infrastructure.

Jaedong was Zerg's greatest champion in Brood War and Flash's greatest rival, with their battles referred to as the 'Tyrant vs. God' rivalry. He won two OSL titles and four MSL titles, and his battles against Flash in finals are considered the greatest rivalry in StarCraft competitive history.

Grubby was the dominant Warcraft III player outside of South Korea, winning multiple World Cyber Games gold medals and the WC3L title multiple times. He later became one of Twitch's most educational StarCraft II streamers, known for his analytical commentary and coaching streams.

Nadeshot was the most recognized Call of Duty competitive player of the early esports era, winning the CoD Championship with OpTic Gaming in 2014. He later founded 100 Thieves, one of the most valuable esports organizations in North America, valued at over $460 million.

Hafu became the most recognized female gaming personality in streaming through her Hearthstone and later Among Us content, consistently placing in the top 100 of Hearthstone's competitive ladder. She was also a professional online poker player before streaming and represents one of the longest-running female gaming careers.

Scump is considered the greatest Call of Duty player in history, having won the Call of Duty World League Championship twice with OpTic Gaming and Chicago Huntsmen. His consistency across nearly a decade of competing at world-championship level established him as the franchise's most enduring competitive personality.

Thijs was among the first dominant Hearthstone players in the game's early competitive era, representing Team Liquid and later G2 Esports to multiple HCT Season wins and Worlds appearances. His analytical streaming approach helped establish Hearthstone as a competitive game deserving serious analysis.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: Moon won the World Cyber Games gold medal four times for Warcraft III, making him the most decorated WCG champion in the game's history. His Night Elf play with mass Dryads and Moon Wells became iconic, and his 2004-2006 dominance of world events established him as the game's greatest champion.

Taeja was the dominant Terran player in StarCraft II's early era, winning multiple WCS and GSL titles in 2012-2013 and holding the world number one ranking. His sustained aggression with Marines and Medivacs inspired a generation of Terran players worldwide.

sp: Super Smash Bros. Melee / Brawl / Ultimate
ach: Mew2King is one of the most technically precise Smash players of all time, consistently ranking in the Melee top 10 for over a decade with Marth and Sheik. His ability to compete across multiple Smash titles simultaneously is unmatched, and he won EVO in Brawl in 2008.

Scarlett is the most successful female esports player in the history of StarCraft II, reaching Grandmaster on Korean servers - the first and one of the only non-Korean Western women to do so. She beat multiple world-ranked Korean players in premier events and represents the highest ceiling of competitive achievement by a female StarCraft player.

Bang is one of the most decorated ADC players in League of Legends history, winning three consecutive World Championships (2015, 2016, 2017) with SK Telecom T1 alongside Faker. He formed what many consider the most dominant bot lane duo in Worlds history with support Wolf, and his precise mechanical play earned consistent top rankings across multiple seasons.

Ryujehong was the most celebrated Overwatch player of the game's inaugural competitive era, leading the Seoul Dynasty to the 2018 Overwatch World Cup victory and being named MVP. His Ana gameplay - particularly his sleeping of key targets - was considered mechanically unmatchable and set the standard for support play in OWL.

Sinatraa won the Overwatch League MVP award in his rookie season with the San Francisco Shock and was the highest-profile player in OWL Season 1. He later transitioned to Valorant where he competed with Sentinels, influencing the game's early competitive meta.
wi: In 2021, his ex-girlfriend publicly accused him of sexual abuse. Riot Games suspended him from competitive Valorant for 6 months during the investigation. He left the pro scene shortly after his reinstatement.

ach: Summit1g was a professional CS:GO player who pivoted to full-time streaming and became one of Twitch's top 5 most-watched streamers for several years. His personality-driven streams across FPS, survival, and roleplay servers made him one of the streaming era's most enduring personalities.

TimTheTatman grew from a World of Warcraft streamer to one of Twitch's top personalities through his infectious personality during Fortnite's peak. He signed an exclusive deal with YouTube Gaming in 2021, and his comedic streaming style expanded gaming content into mainstream entertainment.

Ceb won The International twice with OG in 2018 and 2019, uniquely playing in the offlane position during TI8 and as a support for TI9. His mid-tournament roster change between TI8 and TI9 - dropping to support to accommodate Topson - is one of esports' most celebrated acts of team sacrifice.

ana won The International back-to-back with OG in 2018 and 2019, and is considered the most individually skilled carry player in Dota 2 history. His signature Io carry play at TI8 - choosing the position-5 support hero as a carry in a World Championship final - is the most discussed strategic innovation in Dota history.

Ruler won two League of Legends World Championships with different teams (Samsung Galaxy 2017, Gen.G 2022), making him one of only a handful of players to win Worlds multiple times. His longevity at the ADC position at world-championship level and consistency across teams earns him a place among the greatest Korean ADC players.

Fuudo won the EVO Championship for Super Street Fighter IV: Arcade Edition in 2011, one of the most competitive years in the tournament's history. He remained a top competitor in the Street Fighter circuit for years and is among Japan's most enduring fighting game champions.

Bizzle is recognized as one of the most consistent solo players in Fortnite's competitive history, with multiple FNCS and Cash Cup victories. His longevity at the top of competitive Fortnite despite roster changes and meta shifts makes him one of the title's most respected long-term competitors.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: ZooMaa was FaZe Clan's SMG specialist and is widely regarded as the most mechanically gifted SMG player in Call of Duty competitive history. He won the CoD World League Championship with FaZe Clan and was one of the most feared slayers in the game. Career-ending hand injuries cut short one of CoD's most brilliant playing careers.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: pashaBiceps was the most beloved personality in CS:GO and the face of the legendary Virtus.pro Polish lineup that dominated European competition and won multiple tournament titles. His famous 'big plays' - running AWP kills, jumping shots, and eccentric celebration dances - made him one of esports' most recognized characters globally.

Stats is one of the most successful Protoss players in StarCraft II history, winning the WCS Finals in 2014 and multiple GSL titles. His macro play and late-game decision-making with Protoss units were considered among the most technically perfect in SC2 competitive history, earning him consistent top rankings in Korean Grandmaster league.

Ricki Ortiz is one of the most celebrated female competitive fighting game players in history, winning EVO-caliber events with Chun-Li in Street Fighter IV and becoming a prominent advocate for inclusivity in the fighting game community. Her tournament placements against the world's elite male competitors earned widespread respect.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: Mitr0 was considered the greatest European Fortnite player of his era, winning multiple FNCS championships and becoming the first EU player to achieve global Fortnite solo recognition. He later signed with Natus Vincere as a CS2 player, becoming one of gaming's most successful multi-title professionals bridging battle royale and tactical shooter genres.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: LS built a reputation as the most analytical and controversial League of Legends content creator and coach, known for his deep dive into Korean LoL strategy and his coaching stints with multiple international teams. His detailed breakdown videos transformed how Western audiences understood Korean LoL strategy and macro decision-making.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: Profit was widely regarded as the greatest Tracer player in Overwatch history and won the Overwatch League MVP award in 2018 with the London Spitfire. He and his partner Birdring formed the most dominant DPS duo in the first Overwatch League season, carrying London to the inaugural OWL championship.

Walshy was the most decorated Halo 2 and Halo 3 competitive player, winning multiple MLG National Championships as part of the team Final Boss. He brought credibility to Halo as a legitimate competitive game and his work as an analyst and personality extended his influence well beyond his playing career.

Tsquared won over 50 national gaming championships across Halo and other titles, holding the record for most MLG titles won at one point. He was among the first gamers to pursue a full-time professional esports career in the Halo era and set prize money earning records that stood for years.

Matumbaman won TI7 with Team Liquid and was consistently ranked among the top 5 carry players in Dota 2 throughout his peak years. His partnership with KuroKy was considered among the most effective player-captain relationships in Dota 2 competitive history.

SquishyMuffinz is considered the greatest mechanical Rocket League player of all time, having won three RLCS World Championships with different teams (Cloud9 2017, NRG 2021, NRG 2022). His aerial control and ball-reading ability are considered technically unmatched in the game's history, and his highlight reels revolutionized what players believed was achievable.

ap0calypse was one of the most versatile riflers in Rainbow Six Siege's European competitive scene, competing with multiple top-tier organizations including Luminosity Gaming, Team Secret, and G2 Esports. He was a key figure in establishing Germany as a competitive R6 nation and helped shape the early European Siege meta.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: Crimsix is the most decorated player in Call of Duty Championship history, having won three CoD World Leagues with OpTic Gaming and Dallas Empire. His longevity, professionalism, and adaptability across multiple game titles in the CoD franchise made him one of the franchise's few multi-generational champions.

GorillA is considered one of the greatest supports in League of Legends history, winning the 2017 World Championship with Samsung Galaxy and known for his Rakan and Thresh play in team fights. His synergy with Ruler in the bot lane was described as the best bot lane duo at Worlds 2017.

Wolf was the support for SK Telecom T1 during their dynasty years (2015-2016-2017), forming one of the most decorated support-ADC duos in Worlds history alongside Bang. He contributed to three World Championship wins and is among the few players to win back-to-back-to-back Worlds.

TenZ was considered the greatest mechanical duelist player in Valorant's early competitive history, signing with Sentinels in a deal widely reported at $2-3 million per year. His Jett play during Valorant's formative competitive period set a new standard for aggressive entry-fragging and he became the face of Valorant's esports scene.

ScreaM earned the nickname 'The Headshot Machine' in CS:GO for his extraordinarily high headshot percentage with AK-47 and rifles. He transitioned to Valorant where he replicated his precise mechanical impact, leading Team Liquid to European championship titles and competing at world championships.

Coldzera was HLTV's number one ranked player in both 2016 and 2017, making him the only player in CS:GO history to win the award back-to-back, and led SK Gaming to two consecutive Major Championship titles. His jumping no-scope clip against Team Liquid at MLG Columbus is one of the most replayed moments in CS:GO history.

FalleN captained the Brazilian lineup that broke Korean dominance of CS:GO Majors, winning two consecutive Majors in 2016 with SK Gaming. His dual role as AWPer and in-game leader was considered exceptional even by world-championship standards, and he is credited with developing Brazil's CS:GO professional scene from the ground up.

syndereN is one of esports' most respected analysts and broadcasters, having transitioned from professional Dota 2 play to a long career as a panelist and analyst at The International. His clear explanations of complex strategies helped millions of viewers understand Dota 2 at its highest competitive level.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend

ach: Leffen is one of the few players to have defeated all five of the original 'Gods' of Smash Melee in bracket play and is considered among the top Melee players of all time. His aggressive personality, analytical streaming content, and willingness to compete across multiple Smash titles make him one of the community's most polarizing and celebrated figures.

ElkY was one of the first Western professional StarCraft: Brood War players to compete in Korea before transitioning to professional poker where he became a World Series of Poker bracelet winner. His unique career path - from gaming pro to poker pro - remains one of esports' most remarkable crossover stories.

DrLupo was one of the most respected Fortnite streamers and is known for raising over $2.3 million for St. Jude Children's Research Hospital through charity streams. His family-friendly content, genuine generosity, and high skill ceiling made him one of the most liked streamers across both gaming and mainstream audiences.

Imaqtpie was the most-watched League of Legends streamer on Twitch for multiple years running, building an audience through his casual, comedic style that contrasted with the hyper-competitive tone of most LoL content. He was a former professional LCS player for Team Dignitas who transitioned to content creation.

Jensen was one of North America's most consistent LCS mid laners across multiple years with Cloud9 and Team Liquid, reaching the Worlds semifinals in 2018 with C9 - NA's best Worlds result in a decade. His calm, analytical approach made him one of the most respected players in the NA scene.

Stork was considered the greatest Protoss player in Brood War history, winning two OSL championships and multiple proleague titles with Samsung KHAN. His precise macro management and late-game army control were cited as the highest expression of Protoss gameplay in professional Brood War.

Aspas is widely considered the greatest duelist player in Valorant history, winning the VCT Champions 2022 World Championship with LOUD and being named tournament MVP. His mechanical ceiling is described by professionals as the highest in the game's history, and his emergence helped establish Brazil as a dominant force in global Valorant esports.
nim: 🎮 No image available for this legend
